summary: Completed read-only post-patch audit #14 for installer/release verification. Direct install.ps1 invokes verify:os and verify:codex-loop, and GUI verification defaults on. Findings: release/build self-test does not assert embedded install.ps1 contains verify:codex-loop; SkipVerify bypasses both verifiers yet still completes install and starts hook approval; no-Git CLI docs are inconsistent because verify:codex-loop hard-requires git; release SkipBuild/AssetPath can package stale unverified EXE; default release AppRef=main can drift from release commit.
decisions: Do not edit files for this audit.
Treat direct install.ps1 verification inclusion as present but release/build proof as incomplete.
Treat no-Git default verification as fail-closed rather than silently successful, but document/resolve the contradiction.
next_steps: Add build-windows-installer self-test assertion for verify:codex-loop in embedded install.ps1.
Make SkipVerify visibly unsafe or gated by a stronger explicit flag and status artifact.
Resolve no-Git policy: either require Git upfront everywhere or allow a degraded install only with explicit degraded status and no closed-loop claim.
Have release packaging re-extract and assert embedded verifier content even when SkipBuild or AssetPath is used.
